data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-12T09:04:49.969Z</current_datetime>

Help me write MSTest tests for this formatting utility. I want to use the right MSTest string assertion methods like StartsWith and EndsWith to validate the output formats.

```csharp
namespace Contoso.Text;

public sealed class StringFormatter
{
    public string FormatPhoneNumber(string digits)
    {
        ArgumentNullException.ThrowIfNull(digits);
        if (digits.Length != 10)
            throw new ArgumentException("Must be exactly 10 digits.", nameof(digits));
        return $"({digits[..3]}) {digits[3..6]}-{digits[6..]}";
    }

    public string BuildGreeting(string name, string title = "Mr.")
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(name);
        return $"Dear {title} {name},";
    }

    public string GenerateSlug(string title)
    {
        ArgumentException.ThrowIfNullOrWhiteSpace(title);
        return Regex.Replace(title.Trim().ToLowerInvariant(), @"[^a-z0-9]+", "-").Trim('-');
    }

    public string FormatCurrency(decimal amount, string symbol = "$")
    {
        return $"{symbol}{amount:F2}";
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to create MSTest tests for a `StringFormatter` class. They specifically want me to use MSTest's string assertion methods like `StartsWith` and `EndsWith`. Let me create a well-structured test file.

Let me first check what's in the working directory to understand if there's an existing project structure.

data.reasoningText:
Empty directory. I need to set up a project structure with the source code and tests. Let me create a solution with the source project and a test project.

Let me think about the MSTest string assertions. MSTest v3 has `Assert.That.String()` fluent assertions including:
- `StringAssert.StartsWith(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.EndsWith(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.Contains(string, substring)`
- `StringAssert.Matches(string, regex)`

These are the classic MSTest StringAssert methods. Let me use those.

Let me create the projects and write comprehensive tests.

data.reasoningText: MSTest v3 uses `Assert.ThrowsExactly` instead of `Assert.ThrowsException`. Let me check the MSTest version.

data.content: MSTest 4.x uses `Assert.ThrowsExactly` instead of `Assert.ThrowsException`. Let me fix that:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

data.reasoningText: MSTest v4 uses `Assert.ThrowsExactly<T>` instead of `Assert.ThrowsException<T>`. Let me fix that.

arguments.description: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-dddb4d22dec4485ea00ed87ce5140c5d && sed -i 's/Assert\\.ThrowsException/Assert.ThrowsExactly/g' tests/Contoso.Text.Tests/StringFormatterTests.cs", "description": "Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4"}

toolArgs.description: Fix ThrowsException to ThrowsExactly for MSTest v4

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-dddb4d22dec4485ea00ed87ce5140c5d && dotnet test --no-restore -v quiet 2>&1 | tail -10", "description": "Run tests again", "initial_wait": 60}
